title: Bitwise operators should not be applied to signed operands
description: Bitwise operators should not be applied to signed operandsAnalyze your codeintentionality-logicalreliabilityBugcwebased-on-misralock-inbad-practicecertWhy is this an issue?More InfoMost built-in bitwise operators (~,>>,>>=,&,&=,^,^=,|, and|=) have implementation-dependent results when performed on signed operands, and bitwise left shift (<<and<<=) has unspecified or undefined behavior when performed on negative operands.Therefore, bitwise operations should not be performed on signed operands.Starting with C++20, the behaviors have been defined more accurately (negative values have to be represented using two’s complement), and therefore this rule will only report an issue when the second operand of a shift operator is signed (shifting by a negative value is still undefined behavior).Noncom
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

noncompliant:
int signed_var = -5;
int result = ~signed_var;        // Noncompliant: bitwise NOT on signed operand

int a = -10, b = 3;
int result = a << b;             // Noncompliant: left shift with signed operand

int x = -7;
x >>= 2;                         // Noncompliant: signed right shift assignment

int val = -15;
val &= 0xFF;                  // Noncompliant: bitwise AND assignment with signed operand

signed int mask = 0x0F;
int data = value | mask;      // Noncompliant: bitwise OR with signed operand

int a = -1, b = 5;
int result = a ^ b;              // Noncompliant: XOR with signed first operand
compliant:
unsigned int unsigned_var = 5;
int result = ~unsigned_var;      // Compliant: bitwise NOT on unsigned operand

unsigned int a = 10, b = 3;
int result = a << b;             // Compliant: left shift with unsigned operands

unsigned int x = 7;
x >>= 2;                         // Compliant: unsigned right shift assignment

unsigned int val = 15;
val &= 0xFF;                  // Compliant: bitwise AND assignment with unsigned operand

unsigned int mask = 0x0F;
int data = value | mask;      // Compliant: bitwise OR with unsigned operand

unsigned int a = 1, b = 5;
int result = a ^ b;              // Compliant: XOR with unsigned operands

int a = 10;
int result = a + 5;             // Compliant: arithmetic operation, not bitwise
